name: Simple settings page — tests meaningful variation vs cosmetic shuffling

input.userMessage: Brainstorm variations for this settings page
input.frameDescription: Simple settings page with left sidebar navigation (Profile, Security, Notifications, Billing, Integrations) and right content area showing Profile settings form (avatar upload, name, email, bio textarea, save/cancel buttons)

expectedBehaviors: Each variation uses a DIFFERENT SCAMPER lens (not just rearranging the same elements)
At least one variation challenges the sidebar pattern (Eliminate: what if tabs instead?)
At least one variation changes the HIERARCHY (Modify: what if the most-changed settings are promoted?)
Each variation states which user job it optimizes for (Configure vs Learn vs Act)
Variations are labeled with their SCAMPER lens and rationale
Recommendation is grounded in user behavior, not 'cleaner layout'
antiPatterns: All variations are the same layout with different spacing/colors
Variations only differ in sidebar position (left vs right vs top)
No variation challenges the fundamental IA (all keep sidebar + form)
Recommendation says 'Variation 2 looks cleanest' instead of citing user behavior
Produces 4 variations that are essentially the same with minor tweaks

evaluationCriteria.variationDiversity: Are the variations MEANINGFULLY different in layout/hierarchy/IA?
evaluationCriteria.scamperApplication: Does each variation apply a specific, named SCAMPER lens?
evaluationCriteria.jtbdAlignment: Does each variation state which user job it serves?
evaluationCriteria.recommendationQuality: Is the recommendation grounded in behavior, not aesthetics?
